speaker: narrator
text: This seems like a quiet little town.  On the porch ahead of you are two people.  The standing one is large, rather ugly, and playing with a yo-yo.  The seated person smoking a pipe looks like he might be the Sheriff.

speaker: narrator
text: The Sheriff's office doubles as a jail for the town and a hangout for the Sheriff and the Goon.

speaker: narrator
text: The Sign of the Scissors above the door, along with the striped pole, indicates that the building contains a Barber Shop.

speaker: narrator
text: The windows glow from the light inside. The sign reads "Hero's Tale Inn".

speaker: narrator
text: There are signs over each of the buildings informing you of their purpose.

speaker: narrator
text: The town gate is standing wide open, as it does every day.

speaker: narrator
text: Spielburg remains colorful, even at night.

speaker: narrator
text: The shades are drawn, and it appears that the Inn is closed.

speaker: narrator
text: The town gate is made of sturdy logs.

speaker: narrator
text: The door appears to be locked, and breaking into the Sheriff's office might evoke the wrath of Otto Von Goon.

speaker: narrator
text: You are carrying so much that you can hardly move.  You'd better drop something soon.

speaker: narrator
text: You can't get that.

speaker: narrator
text: There aren't any here.

speaker: narrator
text: You don't have time -- there's the slight matter of the monster that wants to eat you.

speaker: narrator
text: You can't see it.

speaker: narrator
text: Turn around.

speaker: narrator
text: That's the Sheriff's job.

speaker: narrator
text: Searching a law enforcer is a bad idea.

speaker: narrator
text: The door is locked.

speaker: narrator
text: It stands open.

speaker: shameen
text: "Welcome!  Welcome, traveler, to the Hero's Tale Inn!  I am Shameen, at your service.  May you find what you seek here."

speaker: shameen
text: "May you dream of all the rewards you deserve."

speaker: guard
text: "Please step where I can see you."

speaker: guard
text: "If ya want a good time, try the 'Dragon's Breath' at the bar."

speaker: narrator
text:

through the efforts of many people:
designers, artists, programmers, musicians,
and lots of other hard-working folks.

If you make copies of this software for any
reason other than to make a personal backup,
you are not only breaking the law, but raising

PLEASE
DO NOT MAKE ILLEGAL COPIES OF THIS GAME !
